commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7f4d2a1dfed60ac6e995758e3c8443b393251bb0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds abort-reason tagging and helper coverage so embedded agent cleanup suppresses `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on abort signals classified as user-initiated while preserving timeout, restart, and auth-revoked failures.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +66. Total +118 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection shows the current cleanup boundary can propagate session takeover errors after an aborted run, but the PR body does not prove the actual UI Stop-button path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR still needs a positive user-abort contract decision plus contributor-owned real behavior proof that automation cannot supply.

target.title: fix(agents): suppress EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError on user-ini…
target.body:
## Summary

When a user presses the Stop button during agent thinking, the session fence mechanism detects session file modification and throws `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`. This error is injected into the conversation context, polluting the agent's next turn with noise.

This PR identifies user-initiated aborts (via `params.abortSignal?.aborted` **AND** not timeout-based) and suppresses the takeover error from conversation context while still logging it for debugging.

### Key Changes

- Add `shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort()` helper function
- **Fixed logic**: Check `userInitiatedAbort` excludes timeout aborts (`timedOut`, `idleTimedOut`, `timedOutDuringCompaction`)
- Log warning instead of `Promise.reject` for user abort case
- Session fence mechanism still effective (safety boundary preserved)

### Issue Reference

Fixes #88903

### Codex Review Response

Addresses **[P2] Exclude timeout aborts from takeover suppression** finding:
- Original logic treated all `abortSignal.aborted` cases as user-initiated
- Gateway timeout aborts use the same `aborted` signal
- Fix: Only suppress when `abortSignal.aborted && !timedOut && !idleTimedOut && !timedOutDuringCompaction`
- Ensures timeout-based takeover errors remain fatal and visible to downstream handling

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: Suppresses `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` from conversation context when user presses Stop button, preventing error pollution in agent's next turn. Timeout-based aborts still surface takeover errors as fatal (userInitiatedAbort excludes timedOut/idleTimedOut/timedOutDuringCompaction).

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.19+, `07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw` checkout on branch `fix/suppress-session-takeover-error-on-abort-88903`, commit fef4425650aaf1bd70d65ef07b435a14e6bc2ce1.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
# 1. Verify code logic: run shouldSuppressTakeoverErrorOnUserAbort against 3 scenarios
#    Scenario 1: User Stop + Takeover → shouldSuppress = true (clean abort)
#    Scenario 2: Timeout + Takeover → shouldSuppress = false (fatal preserved)
#    Scenario 3: User Stop + Other Error → shouldSuppress = false (narrow predicate)

# 2. Run unit test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts --run
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
Scenario 1 (User Stop + Takeover Error):  shouldSuppress = true  ✓
Scenario 2 (Timeout + Takeover Error):    shouldSuppress = false ✓
Scenario 3 (User Stop + Other Error):     shouldSuppress = false ✓
All scenarios PASS.

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668000363/requre-an/MAAS-MR/memory-work-main/07 Open Source/02-projects/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts (42 tests) 462ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  42 passed (42)
   Duration  1.29s (transform 360ms, setup 394ms, import 140ms, tests 462ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 10.95s
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- User Stop + Takeover Error: suppressed from conversation (log.warn only), agent next turn stays clean
- Timeout + Takeover Error: NOT suppressed, error still injected into conversation (fatal preserved)
- User Stop + Other Error: NOT suppressed, unaffected by this change
- Session fence detection remains active for both abort types
- All 42 existing tests pass with no regressions

**What was not tested**: Live UI Stop button press in actual OpenClaw dashboard (requires running dev gateway), full agent runner workflow with real provider/model calls, multi-agent concurrent access scenarios.

---

**Related issues**: #87966, #88369, #88457, #88471, #88653 (distinct from this fix - those address false positives, this addresses UX impact of legitimate user abort)
target.author: tanshanshan

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
